mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered months or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during economic expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of co-expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production indicate immediate supply-side bottlenecks and earnings revision risks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day returns in > 50% of contraction months or shows no predictive edge during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or 60-day forward returns show no positive skew during payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 100,000 units signals a sharp pullback in construction demand and related consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered months or loses predictive power during housing market downturns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates labor market slack being absorbed by manufacturing, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances or the mechanism shows no edge during recovery phases.
